data_status: research communication data with a validated synthetic-population subset
dsm_context_status: normalized FSA-level DSM alignment outputs and energy-demand indicators for 94 Montreal FSAs
disclaimer: This is a research communication demo using simplified data. It illustrates the workflow and findings, not an operational DSM recommendation tool.

source_repositories_reviewed.name: dsm-alignment
reused_concepts: Flex D, Hilo, LogisVert, and low-income alignment constructs
area-level demand relevance versus suitability/capacity framing
small normalized FSA-level alignment-score excerpt for the app baseline

limitations: The v1-demo Git tag preserves the eSim-only conference release; v2 adds a synthetic-population exploration layer.
The complete linked person-household bundle currently covers 30 dissemination areas in H2J. Other FSAs expose five compact profiles sampled from a Montreal-wide synthetic population artifact.
Resident-level scores are illustrative household-context overlays, not validated individual predictions or program recommendations.
DSM alignment remains an FSA-level analysis. Synthetic people and households add heterogeneity for exploration without changing the geographic scale of the program evidence.
